picks.title: Solstice Festival
picks.blurb: Summer solstice celebration with music, art, performances & rituals marking longest day.

picks.title: Guns N' Roses + Mammoth
picks.blurb: Rock legends headline Amsterdam's biggest venue. Mammoth opens; main stage 19:30.
picks.why: Arena rock spectacle, likely selling fast

picks.title: Lofi Courtyard: Electronic Music Festival
picks.blurb: All-day electronic courtyard festival. Bella Claxton, DJ Frank, Jenny Cara, NewTone, Schwesta P b2b Tjade.
picks.why: Early afternoon start, sun-drenched outdoor setting

picks.title: John Summit + Mesto, Guz
picks.blurb: Electronic music on Sunday afternoon in open courtyard with Chicago house DJ John Summit.
picks.why: Sold out; daylight electronic outdoor event

picks.title: Metamorphoses & FAKE!
picks.blurb: 80+ masterpieces inspired by Ovid's myths, plus early photomanipulation exhibition. Closes June 21.

picks.title: Guns N' Roses and Mammoth WVH
picks.blurb: Stadium rock legends bring massive production to Amsterdam's premier indoor arena this Saturday.

picks.title: Phantogram
picks.blurb: Electronic-art-rock duo perform at iconic Amsterdam venue, Friday night opener for the weekend.

picks.title: Free Sunset Yoga
picks.blurb: Free outdoor yoga session at sunset, mindfulness and movement as sun sets on solstice.

picks.title: Queer Pop Party (Absoluut + TMORGZ)
picks.blurb: Amsterdam's resident queer pop party with 90s–today pop hits. Running the whole weekend.
picks.why: Extended weekend run, eclectic pop curation

picks.title: Rebecca Rue | SHIFFT (Rooted in Motion & Brother)
picks.blurb: Dance performances exploring tension between form and fluidity, with recreation of a life touched by loss.

picks.title: LuminAir Father's Day Special
picks.blurb: Rooftop bar with panoramic views celebrates Father's Day with complimentary whisky cocktails for dads; terrace, Disaronno menu, daytime to late.

picks.title: Hortus Botanicus Music on Sunday
picks.blurb: Botanical garden stays open late (until 20:00) with live evening concert; June Sundays only with blooming borders, tropical greenhouses.
picks.why: Summer evening music series in golden hour garden

picks.title: Sunset Yoga Session
picks.blurb: Free sunset yoga to end the longest day of the year. Organised by David Lloyd Clubs Amsterdam.

picks.title: Therapeutic Flying Workshop
picks.blurb: Breathwork and movement session for personal development. Limited to 16 participants. Wear comfortable clothes.

picks.title: Port Festival / Amsterdam Seaport Days
picks.blurb: Guided ship tours, demonstrations, free boat rides, activities for all ages at the waterfront.

picks.title: Sunset Yoga on International Yoga Day
picks.blurb: Free outdoor yoga session organized by David Lloyd Clubs for summer solstice celebration.
picks.source: Your Little Black Book / Barts Boekje

picks.title: Eye(s) Open: New Perspectives on Colonial Film Heritage
picks.blurb: Eleven contemporary artists respond to Eye's colonial-era Indonesian and Surinamese films with bold new works exploring this charged archive.

picks.title: World Press Photo
picks.blurb: Iconic photography exhibition showcasing the year's most powerful journalistic images from around the globe.
picks.why: Prestigious annual exhibition, runs through summer

picks.title: MOCO Museum Late Evening
picks.blurb: Contemporary art museum (Banksy, Warhol) open daily until 21:00. Quieter evening browsing before clubs.

picks.title: Bite of Amsterdam 2026
picks.blurb: Annual food festival celebrating Dutch and international cuisine across the city with tastings, cooking demos, and vendors.
picks.why: Major food event, three-day foodie destination
picks.source: Your Little Black Book · Bite of Amsterdam official · Bite of Amsterdam / I amsterdam · I amsterdam / Bite of Amsterdam · I amsterdam

picks.title: Bacchus Winefestival
picks.blurb: Amsterdam's oldest wine event showcases finest wineries with tastings, regional dishes, and storytelling across three summer weekends.
picks.why: Final weekend of June's festival rotation
picks.source: I amsterdam · Amsterdam Experience · Amsterdam Spotted / Barts Boekje · Amsterdam Spotted / I amsterdam · Amsterdamian

picks.title: Holland Festival 2026
picks.blurb: 79th edition of Europe's leading international performing arts festival with theatre, dance, music, opera, multidisciplinary work.
picks.source: I amsterdam · Holland Festival / I amsterdam · Songkick

picks.title: Open Garden Days
picks.blurb: Rare chance to explore picturesque private canal garden hideaways normally closed to public; 19-21 June only this year.
picks.why: One weekend only, access to hidden gardens
picks.source: I amsterdam & Open Tuinen Dagen · I amsterdam · I Amsterdam

picks.title: Carel Visser — sculpture in the gardens
picks.blurb: Free outdoor sculpture show — the first artist solo in the Rijksmuseum gardens, opening this weekend.

picks.title: Museum Market
picks.blurb: Arts, crafts, local design and food stalls with live music on Amsterdam's grandest plaza.

picks.title: 24 Hours Nieuw-West
picks.blurb: Day-long neighbourhood celebration with street art tours, brewery visits, bird watching, DJ sets, museum parties and more.

picks.title: The Movies — arthouse cinema
picks.blurb: The Netherlands’ oldest cinema still running — Art Deco rooms, a dinner-and-a-film deal, arthouse bills.
picks.why: Indoor · dinner + a film · perfect when it’s wet

picks.title: Ed van der Elsken Photography
picks.blurb: Dutch photographer's 20th-century images opening this weekend—intimate, street-level view of Amsterdam.

picks.title: Yumna Al-Arashi — Body as Resistance
picks.blurb: First solo museum show for Al-Arashi — photography, sculpture and book confront the policing of women’s bodies.

picks.title: East Beach — rooftop city beach
picks.blurb: A new 800m² rooftop city beach above Oosterpark — sand, beach chairs, drinks, open to all.

picks.title: De Pimpelmees
picks.blurb: Cosy neighbourhood wine bar — European wines from €6, Italian snacks, free chips. Opened early 2026.

picks.title: GRIMM
picks.blurb: Hip-hop and ballet collide in a spectacular, darkly funny take on fairy tales for families aged 8+.
picks.why: Critics' pick: dazzling twist on classic stories

picks.title: Havenfestival 2026
picks.blurb: Free waterfront fun: harbour activities, demonstrations, and family-friendly entertainment across the island.
picks.why: No-cost weekend action for all ages

picks.title: Bite of Amsterdam Food Festival
picks.blurb: Global cuisine, free masterclasses, live music. Extra focus on vegetarian options and families.
picks.source: I Amsterdam / Your Little Black Book

picks.title: Spinoza — De Mokum Musical
picks.blurb: Open-air musical about philosopher Spinoza set in old Amsterdam, in the forest theatre.
picks.why: Cross-source (LBB + I amsterdam) · open-air · dry-night plan

picks.title: Eye Classics
picks.blurb: Film history on the big screen: curated classics screened in 35mm whenever possible, from cinema's early days to now.

picks.title: Zaanse Schans windmills
picks.blurb: Eight working windmills, cheese and crafts on the Zaan — free grounds, ~17 min train from Centraal.
picks.why: Short train · outdoor · classic kid day

picks.title: Haarlem — the easy escape
picks.blurb: A calmer historic city 15 min away — Grote Markt, the St-Bavo organ, a Saturday market and café-lined square.

picks.title: Foodhallen
picks.blurb: An indoor food hall in a converted tram depot — 20-odd stalls, long tables and two bars under one roof.
picks.why: Indoor · grazing for a crowd · weather-proof

picks.title: Moeders — Dutch home cooking
picks.blurb: Walls papered with strangers’ mothers’ photos and a stamppot-and-stew menu built for a grey evening.
picks.why: Snug · classic Dutch · ideal in the rain

picks.title: Café de Klos — ribs & grill
picks.blurb: A tiny, packed grill house off the canals famous for sticky spare ribs — no frills, no reservations.
picks.why: Warm · cheap · duck in out of the wet

picks.title: Wynand Fockink — jenever tasting
picks.blurb: A 1679 tasting room behind Dam Square pouring its own jenevers and liqueurs — you bend to the brimful glass.
picks.why: Historic · indoor · made for a downpour

picks.title: Café Chris — oldest brown café
picks.blurb: Open since 1624: a creaky, golden-lit Jordaan brown café for a slow afternoon beer while it pours outside.

picks.title: Eye Filmmuseum — repertory screening
picks.blurb: Across the IJ by free ferry: a striking film museum with a daily repertory programme and a river-view café.
picks.why: Indoor · a film + the ferry ride · weather-proof

picks.title: Sauna Deco — Art Deco baths
picks.blurb: A 1920s Art Deco bathhouse (salvaged from a Paris department store) with saunas, steam and a quiet tea room.
picks.why: Warm · restorative · the ultimate cold-and-wet antidote

picks.title: Pllek — waterfront hang
picks.blurb: A cargo-container café-bar on the NDSM wharf — big windows onto the IJ, sofas, and a wood stove when it turns.
picks.why: Indoor seating · IJ views · cozy when grey

picks.title: Tony’s Chocolonely Super Store
picks.blurb: The flagship of the slavery-free chocolate brand — make-your-own bars, a café, and a serious sweet-tooth detour.
picks.why: Indoor · kid-friendly · a happy rainy hour

picks.title: Madurodam
picks.blurb: The whole of the Netherlands at 1:25 — miniature canals, Schiphol and windmills kids can tower over.

picks.title: Efteling
picks.blurb: Europe’s oldest fairytale theme park — enchanted forest, dark rides and proper coasters in a storybook world.

picks.title: As
picks.blurb: Wood-fired, ingredient-led cooking and natural wine in a striking round garden pavilion.

picks.title: BAK
picks.blurb: A light-filled warehouse with an open kitchen and an art-covered wall; a set seasonal menu of produce-driven dishes.

picks.title: The Frozen Fountain
picks.blurb: The country’s best-known design store — floors of furniture, lighting and covetable objects to browse.

picks.title: Brewery Homeland
picks.blurb: A craft brewery and beach bar on the IJ — tank-fresh beer and a waterfront sun-trap terrace.

picks.title: De Filmhallen
picks.blurb: A nine-screen arthouse cinema in a converted tram depot — with a bustling food hall right next door.
picks.why: Arthouse · in the old tram depot

picks.title: Vlieger
picks.blurb: A two-floor temple to paper since 1869 — hundreds of colours, handmade sheets and card stacked to the ceiling.

picks.title: Athenaeum Boekhandel
picks.blurb: The intellectual heart of the Spui — a beloved bookshop and an international newsstand packed floor-to-ceiling.

picks.title: The American Book Center
picks.blurb: A sprawling three-floor English-language bookshop — a spiral of shelves and a basement of zines and comics.

picks.title: Patta
picks.blurb: The homegrown streetwear label and culture hub — drops, sneakers and the community that put Amsterdam on the map.

picks.title: Moise
picks.blurb: An understated De Pijp boutique with a razor-sharp eye — Acne, Lemaire, Marant and the like, hand-picked and beautifully shown.

picks.title: Cowboys to Catwalk
picks.blurb: A treasure-trove of vintage designer — Margiela, Marc Jacobs and one-off finds rummaged from decades of fashion.

picks.title: Mobilia
picks.blurb: Three floors of high design on the Utrechtsestraat — icons by the masters alongside more attainable covetables.
